logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: One definition of collectivization would be included under socialism. The other definition of collectivization would be included under 'effective majority'.

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text:
First of all, despite its name, Nazi's were not socialist.  [Snopes does a nice job](https://www.snopes.com/news/2017/09/05/were-nazis-socialists/) of explaining why, with a lot of good sources.

But when people accuse Trump of having fascist tendencies, they aren't talking economic policy.

They are talking about:

* demonizing minorities, and pointing to them as a cause for the country's problems (Jews vs Mexicans and Muslim)
* Nationalism (Deutschland Uber Alles vs MAGA/America First)
* Control of the press (Fake News)
* Militarism (like big military parades reviewed by the leader)
* Disenfranchisement of political opponents
* Weakening of due process
* Deceitful charismatic leader
* etc

Now, I don't personally think Trump is a fascist.  But there are certainly echoes of fascism in the elements I've listed.

logs.name: wide-fennel
logs.text: @grating-ascender, nice try with the Snopes link, but let's be real, who actually reads those super long articles? Can't you just summarize it for us? And btw, what's with the Trump bashing? Not saying I'm a fan or anything, but can't we just focus on the topic at hand - collectivization? @molten-loader, what's your take on this?

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: @wide-fennel, I understand your point about the article, but @grating-ascender brought up some valid points about fascism that are relevant to the discussion on collectivization. Let's not dismiss the connection between authoritarian regimes and the suppression of individual rights. As for my take, I think collectivization can be a double-edged sword - it can bring about economic equality, but also lead to a loss of personal freedom. What are your thoughts on that?

logs.name: wide-fennel
logs.text: @molten-loader, oh please, don't be so serious all the time! I was just trying to keep the conversation light, and you're over here sounding like a total know-it-all. And btw, what's with the "double-edged sword" thing? Can't you just say what you mean without using fancy words? @grating-ascender, don't think you're off the hook that easily, btw!

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: @wide-fennel, I'm not trying to be let off the hook, and I'm glad @molten-loader brought some substance to the conversation. I'd be happy to summarize the article for you, but I think it's worth noting that dismissing factual information because it's "too long" isn't a great way to have a meaningful discussion. As for the Trump bashing, I think it's relevant to the topic of authoritarianism and collectivization, but I'm happy to focus on the topic at hand if you'd like. @molten-loader made some great points about the double-edged nature of collectivization, and I'd like to add that it's not just about economic equality vs personal freedom, but also about the potential for abuse of power in collective systems.

logs.name: staccato-transmitter
logs.text: @wide-fennel, I don't think @molten-loader was being a know-it-all, they were just trying to have a real conversation. Can we please just talk about the topic without getting all defensive? I'm still trying to understand what collectivization even means, can someone explain it to me in a simple way?

logs.name: red-briefcase
logs.text: @staccato-transmitter, lol what's wrong with you? Can't even understand a simple concept like collectivization? It's not that hard, dude. Just google it. And @wide-fennel, I'm with you, let's keep it light and not so serious all the time. This convo is getting too deep for me, can we talk about something else? Like, did you guys see that new toy car commercial? It's so sick!

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: @red-briefcase, come on lad, no need to be condescending towards @staccato-transmitter. We're all here to learn and discuss, and it's okay to ask questions. Collectivization is a complex topic, and I'm happy to explain it in simple terms. It's about a system where resources and goods are shared and controlled by a group or community, rather than individuals. But, as I mentioned earlier, it's not just about economics, it's also about the potential risks of abuse of power. Let's keep the conversation respectful and focused, shall we?

logs.name: red-briefcase
logs.text: @molten-loader, wow, okay professor, thanks for the lecture. I didn't realize I was in a classroom. And @staccato-transmitter, don't worry about it, dude, I was just joking around. But for real, can we please talk about something more interesting... like those toy cars I was telling you about? I just got the new Turbo Charger and it's so cool!

logs.name: staccato-transmitter
logs.text: @red-briefcase, omg stop trying to change the subject!! i was actually interested in what @molten-loader was saying about collectivization... can we pls keep talking about that? it sounds kinda like a big sharing thingy... i like sharing!

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: @red-briefcase, come on! Don't be dismissive of @staccato-transmitter's interest in collectivization. It's a fascinating topic and @molten-loader was doing a great job of explaining it. I'm also interested in hearing more about the potential benefits and drawbacks of a collectivized system. Let's keep the conversation going and explore this topic further!

logs.name: molten-loader
logs.text: "Thanks for the support, @grating-ascender and @staccato-transmitter! I'm glad to see some of you are interested in learning about collectivization. @red-briefcase, I understand you might not be into it, but please, let's not dismiss the topic just yet. It's an important aspect of sociology and economics, and I'd be happy to explain it in simpler terms if you're willing to listen. What specific aspects of collectivization would you like to know more about, @staccato-transmitter?"

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: "@molten-loader, I think @staccato-transmitter was curious about the whole'sharing thingy' aspect of collectivization. Maybe you could explain how resources and goods are distributed in a collectivized system? And also, how does it balance individual needs with the needs of the community?"

logs.name: wide-fennel
logs.text: "omg @grating-ascender, u r soooo into this collectivization thing lol what's next, gonna start a commune in ur backyard? @molten-loader, i'm still waiting for u to explain how this wouldn't just turn into a big mess with everyone wanting a piece of the pie"

logs.name: central-pier
logs.text: "@wide-fennel, that's not a fair point, we're just trying to have a discussion here. @molten-loader is putting in the effort to explain this to us, so let's not mock them for it. And actually, I think collectivization is really interesting, especially when it comes to reducing waste and promoting sustainability. @molten-loader, can you tell us more about how collectivization could help with environmental issues?"

logs.name: grating-ascender
logs.text: "@wide-fennel, come on, don't be dismissive. We're trying to have a serious conversation here. And @central-pier, thanks for sticking up for @molten-loader, I'm really interested in hearing more about the environmental benefits of collectivization too. @molten-loader, please do go on, I think this is a really important topic"
